name: Kettlebell Swing
resistance_profile: Ballistic hip hinge; the bell's momentum drives the posterior chain hardest through the mid-range hip extension, with a stretch load on the hamstrings as the hips reverse at the bottom.
cues: The bell floats — don't lift it with the shoulders
Flat back, chest proud through the hinge
execution_steps: Stand with feet about shoulder-width apart and the kettlebell on the floor roughly a foot in front of you.
Hinge at the hips and hike the bell back between your legs like a football snap, keeping the back flat.
Snap the hips forward explosively, squeezing the glutes so the bell floats up to about chest height on momentum, not arm effort.
Let the bell fall, absorb it by hinging the hips back again, and flow straight into the next rep.
good_when: You want a hip hinge that also builds power and conditioning
Equipment is limited — one kettlebell trains the whole posterior chain
You want posterior-chain work with less spinal compression than heavy barbell hinges
bad_when: You have not yet learned to hinge (default to RDLs first)
You want slow, controlled tension for pure hypertrophy
common_errors: Squatting the bell up and down instead of hinging
Rounding the lower back at the bottom
Using the arms and shoulders to lift the bell
